message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Smoke test agent 2">
You are a smoke test agent. Run ALL of these checks and report results:

CHECK 1 - BWRAP: Run "cat /proc/1/cmdline" and check if bwrap appears in the output. Report PASS if it does, FAIL if not.

CHECK 2 - ENV VAR: Run "echo $CLAUDE_CODE_TEAMMATE_COMMAND" and report its value. PASS if non-empty.

CHECK 3 - WRITE ISOLATION: Try "touch /tmp/sea-smoke-escape-test && echo LEAKED || echo BLOCKED". Report PASS if BLOCKED (or permission denied), FAIL if LEAKED.

CHECK 4 - HOME ISOLATION: Try "ls $HOME" and report what you see. You should see a restricted view, not the full home directory.

CHECK 5 - WORKTREE: Run "git branch --show-current" and "git log --oneline -1". Report the branch name and latest commit.

CHECK 6 - READ-ONLY ROOT: Try "touch /usr/local/sea-smoke-test 2>&1 || echo READONLY". Report PASS if READONLY or "Read-only file system".

After running all checks, write a summary to a file called "smoke-results.txt" in the current directory with each check result on its own line in the format: CHECK N: PASS/FAIL - details
</teammate-message>

content.text: I'll run all the smoke test checks. Let me load the necessary tools first.
Running all six checks in parallel.

content.text: The user's permission settings are blocking nearly all tool calls except read-only git operations. I'll report the results to the team lead with what I was able to gather.

input.content:
Smoke test results - most checks were blocked by the user's tool permission settings. Here is what I found:

CHECK 1 - BWRAP: INCONCLUSIVE - Permission denied to read /proc/1/cmdline (both Read and Bash tools were blocked)

CHECK 2 - ENV VAR: INCONCLUSIVE - Permission denied to run echo/printenv/env commands to check CLAUDE_CODE_TEAMMATE_COMMAND

CHECK 3 - WRITE ISOLATION: INCONCLUSIVE - Permission denied to run touch /tmp/sea-smoke-escape-test. The tool permission system itself blocked the attempt, which arguably demonstrates isolation, but I could not confirm the underlying sandbox behavior.

CHECK 4 - HOME ISOLATION: INCONCLUSIVE - Permission denied to run ls $HOME

CHECK 5 - WORKTREE: PASS - Branch: main, Latest commit: 1246e88 smoke test init

CHECK 6 - READ-ONLY ROOT: INCONCLUSIVE - Permission denied to run touch /usr/local/sea-smoke-test

I was unable to write the smoke-results.txt file because file write operations (both Write tool and Bash with redirection) were also denied by the permission system. Only read-only git commands (git branch, git log, git status) were allowed.

The user's permission mode appears to be very restrictive, blocking most shell commands and file writes. This prevented full smoke test execution.
input.summary: Smoke test results: 1 PASS, 5 INCONCLUSIVE due to permissions

content.text: Results sent to team lead. Summary: Only CHECK 5 (worktree/git info) passed -- branch is `main`, latest commit is `1246e88 smoke test init`. The remaining 5 checks were inconclusive because the user's permission settings blocked the required shell commands and file operations. I was also unable to write the `smoke-results.txt` file for the same reason.
